input:
As the people left, he stood alone on the hill, realizing that he now faced the whole world with no human support apart from the three dozen or so people who had responded favourably to the new call. He realized that the path ahead was an uphill struggle which might involve a conflict with his nearest and dearest. Taken in the context of the tribal Arabian society at the time, this must have been very hard for Muhammad (peace be upon him).
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 7: The Call to Islam Goes Public

output.scene_title: Muhammad Stands Alone on the Hill
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 7: The Call to Islam Goes Public

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Mid-40s, dark olive skin, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing simple white thobe, plain brown turban wrapped around head. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by humble clothing and dignified posture.

output.moral_lesson: Standing alone with faith and conviction can be the greatest strength in a world of opposition.
output.narrative_text: After the people left, Muhammad stood alone on the hill, knowing that he had no human support apart from a few people who believed in his message. He realized that he would have to face the whole world alone, which was a very hard thing to do.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, no human figures in the scene. Simple, peaceful landscape with no conflict. Appropriate for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. A serene 7th century Makkah hillside with rocky outcroppings and scrubby desert plants. A figure in simple white thobe, brown turban, standing alone against the backdrop of the Arabian Desert, with the sun setting behind him. No human figures in the scene. Simple, peaceful palette. No modern objects.

metadata.chapter_title: The Call to Islam Goes Public
